# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: compute-engine-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/e2-medium
    disks:
      - initializeParams:
          sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
        autoDelete: true
        boot: true
    networkInterfaces:
      - network: global/networks/default
        accessConfigs:
          - name: External NAT
            type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT

- name: cloud-sql-instance
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instances
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    settings:
      tier: db-f1-micro
      databaseVersion: MYSQL_5_7
    instanceType: CLOUD_SQL_INSTANCE
    region: us-central1

- name: cloud-storage-bucket
  type: storage.v1.buckets
  properties:
    location: US
    storageClass: STANDARD

- name: load-balancer-target-pool
  type: compute.v1.targetPools
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    instances:
      - $(ref.compute-engine-instance.selfLink)
    healthChecks:
      - $(ref.health-check.selfLink)

- name: health-check
  type: compute.v1.httpHealthChecks
  properties:
    checkIntervalSec: 5
    healthyThreshold: 2
    timeoutSec: 5
    unhealthyThreshold: 2
    requestPath: /
    port: 80

- name: forwarding-rule
  type: compute.v1.forwardingRules
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    target: $(ref.load-balancer-target-pool.selfLink)
    ipProtocol: TCP
    portRange: 80
